title: BookMyShow Merge Show Schedule Overlaps
description: BookMyShow's screen-allocation team has a list of show time-windows for one screen as `[start, end]` pairs (minutes from midnight). Some shows overlap due to last-minute reschedules. Merge every overlapping/touching pair so the screen calendar is clean. Return the merged intervals sorted by start ascending.
hints: Pehle start ke according sort kar de.
Ek result list rakh — har naya interval previous ke saath compare kar.
Agar current.start <= previous.end toh end ko max le, warna naya push kar.

title: Zomato Insert New Delivery Slot
description: Zomato has a sorted, non-overlapping list of delivery time slots `[start, end]` for a partner. A new slot `newSlot` arrives; insert it into the schedule, merging any overlaps. Return the resulting schedule sorted by start.
constraints: slots is sorted by start, non-overlapping
hints: Three phases: pre (entirely before new), merge (overlapping), post (entirely after).
Pre: end < newSlot.start. Post: start > newSlot.end.
Merge phase me start = min, end = max le.

title: Swiggy Partner Shift Conflict Check
description: Swiggy assigns shifts `[start, end]` to a delivery partner. A partner can attend all assigned shifts only if no two overlap (touching at endpoints is fine: shift `[1,3]` and `[3,5]` are OK because handover happens instantly). Return `true` if all shifts can be attended without conflict, else `false`.
hints: Sort by start.
Adjacent compare — agar prev.end > curr.start toh conflict.
Touching (==) allow karna hai problem ke hisaab se.

title: Swiggy Minimum Meeting Rooms
description: Swiggy HQ books many meetings as `[start, end]`. Find the minimum number of conference rooms needed so every meeting can run without conflict. A meeting ending at `t` and another starting at `t` can share the room (instant handover).
hints: Sweep line: +1 at start, -1 at end. Max prefix sum = answer.
Sort starts and ends separately, two pointers — equivalent.
End === Start ko end pehle process kar (handover allowed).

title: Razorpay Min Subscription Removals
description: Razorpay has a list of active subscription billing periods `[start, end]`. To switch a customer to a clean non-overlapping plan, remove the minimum number of periods so no two remaining intervals overlap. Periods touching at endpoints are NOT considered overlapping. Return the count of removals.
hints: Greedy by earliest END time — classic activity selection.
Track last kept end; if next.start < last.end, drop it (count++).
Sort by end ascending; break ties by start.

title: Flipkart Sale-Window Intersections
description: Flipkart's pricing engine has two sorted, non-overlapping interval lists representing two products' sale windows. Compute the list of time windows during which BOTH products are on sale (intersection). Return intersections sorted by start ascending. Touching at a single point counts (closed intervals).
constraints: Each list sorted by start, non-overlapping, ends >= starts
hints: Two pointers, one per list.
Intersection = [max(starts), min(ends)] if start <= end.
Advance pointer with the smaller end.

title: BookMyShow Min Arrows to Burst Promo Balloons
description: At a BookMyShow promo event, balloons hang along a number line; each balloon spans a horizontal interval `[xstart, xend]`. An arrow shot vertically at coordinate `x` bursts every balloon with `xstart <= x <= xend`. Find the minimum arrows to burst them all.
hints: Sort by xend ascending.
Greedy: shoot at the smallest xend — bursts every balloon overlapping it.
Next arrow when next balloon's xstart > previous arrow x.

title: PhonePe Employee Free Time
description: PhonePe wants to schedule a company-wide standup. Given each employee's busy intervals as `schedules` (a flat array where every element is one busy interval `[start, end]`), find the common free time — gaps between the union of all busy intervals. Exclude time before the first busy interval and after the last. Return free intervals sorted by start.

examples.explanation: Union of busy = [1,5] ∪ [6,7] ∪ [9,12]. Gaps inside are [5,6] and [7,9].

hints: Saare intervals ko ek list me daal — merge kar — fir adjacent gaps padh.
Sort by start, sweep and merge.
Touching intervals ka gap zero hai — skip.

title: Ola Carpool Capacity Check
description: An Ola carpool driver has fixed `capacity` seats. Each trip is `[numPassengers, pickupLocation, dropLocation]` along a 1D route (pickup < drop). Determine if every trip can be served without ever exceeding `capacity` at any point. Passengers drop off at exactly `dropLocation` (the seats free up at that location, so a new pickup at the same point can use them).

examples.explanation: Between locations 3 and 5 we'd carry 5 passengers, exceeding 4.

hints: Sweep line on locations: +pax at pickup, -pax at drop.
Sort events; same location: process drops before pickups.
Track running sum; if it exceeds capacity, return false.

title: Swiggy Remove Covered Shift Intervals
description: Swiggy's shift planner has a list of partner shifts `[start, end]`. A shift `[c,d]` is *covered* by another shift `[a,b]` iff `a <= c` and `d <= b` (and they aren't identical to a previous one — duplicates count as covered by the first occurrence). Return the count of shifts that remain after removing every covered shift.
hints: Sort by start asc; tie-break by end DESC (longer first).
Track lastEnd seen — if current.end <= lastEnd it's covered.
Else count it and update lastEnd.

title: Zomato Find Right Delivery Slot
description: Zomato has `n` delivery slots `[start, end]` with unique starts. For each slot `i`, find the index `j` of the slot whose `start` is the smallest value `>= slots[i].end`. If no such slot exists, use `-1`. Return an array `result` where `result[i] = j`.

examples.explanation: [3,4] has no right; [2,3]'s right is index 0 (start 3); [1,2]'s right is index 1 (start 2).

hints: Binary search on a sorted-by-start copy.
Keep original index alongside start.
Lower-bound on `slots[i].end`.

title: Razorpay Active Subscriptions At Time T
description: Razorpay tracks each subscription's active interval `[start, end)` (half-open: active from `start` inclusive to `end` exclusive). For a given timestamp `t`, return how many subscriptions are active at exactly time `t`.

examples.explanation: All three are active at t=4.

hints: Linear count: start <= t < end.
For many queries, precompute sorted starts/ends and use binary search.
Half-open: end == t means inactive.

title: Flipkart Range Addition Page Views
description: Flipkart's analytics gets a list of bursts `[startSecond, endSecond, increment]` (inclusive seconds). Apply each burst to a counter array of length `n` indexed `0..n-1`: every index in `[startSecond, endSecond]` gets `+increment`. Return the final counter array.

examples.explanation: Apply each burst's increment to the inclusive index range.

hints: Diff array trick: diff[start] += inc, diff[end+1] -= inc.
Prefix sum the diff to get the answer.
Watch boundaries — end+1 may equal n; size diff as n+1.

title: BookMyShow Bold Highlight Movie Title
description: BookMyShow's UI wants to bold every occurrence of any keyword from `words` inside a movie title `s`. Wrap consecutive bolded characters in a single `...` tag (overlapping/adjacent matches must merge into one tag). Return the resulting string.
hints: Har word ke saare match positions nikaal — intervals [i, i+len-1].
Merge intervals (touching ko bhi merge kar — adjacent tags ek hi tag me).
Walk s with merged intervals to build output.

title: PhonePe Max Non-Overlapping Promos
description: PhonePe runs promos as `[start, end, value]`. A promo is *active* during `[start, end)` (half-open). Pick a subset of non-overlapping promos that maximises total `value`. Return that maximum total. Two promos with `prevEnd == nextStart` are non-overlapping.
hints: Sort by end. dp[i] = best total using first i promos (sorted).
For each i, binary-search the largest j with end[j] <= start[i] — pred(i).
dp[i] = max(dp[i-1], dp[pred(i)] + value[i]).
